title: planchonella chrysophylloides
intro: Planchonella chrysophylloides, also known as the Blue Satinwood or Blue Satin Tree, is a species of plant in the family Sapotaceae, native to New Caledonia. This plant has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains flavonoids and other compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Planchonella chrysophylloides include its potential in treating various conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its use as a treatment for wounds and skin irritations. Herbal preparations of Planchonella chrysophylloides are typically made from the bark and leaves, which can be used to make teas, infusions, and topical applications.
